seoTitle: How to Verify Your Cloudflare Account Email (2026)
metaDescription: Resend and complete your Cloudflare account email verification to unlock admin features like adding team members and generating API tokens.
intro: Cloudflare locks certain administrative features, like inviting team members or generating API tokens, until your account email is verified. This guide covers finding your verification status, resending an activation email, and troubleshooting when the link doesn't seem to work.

steps.heading: Check your verification status
steps.body: Go to your Cloudflare user profile area and check the email address row for a pending verification indicator.

steps.heading: Resend the verification email
steps.body: Click the 'Send Verification Email' button to have Cloudflare send a fresh activation link to your inbox.

steps.heading: Check your inbox and spam folder
steps.body: Log in to your email provider and search for the activation message, checking spam filters if it doesn't appear right away.

steps.heading: Open the link in a clean browser session
steps.body: Click the activation link, ideally in a private or incognito window, to avoid conflicts with existing session cookies.

steps.heading: Confirm your unlocked status
steps.body: Log back into Cloudflare and check your profile to confirm the account now shows as verified, along with unlocked admin controls.

faq.question: What features are locked until I verify my Cloudflare email?
faq.answer: Verification typically unlocks administrative actions like adding team members and generating API tokens on the account.

faq.question: Why isn't the verification email showing up in my inbox?
faq.answer: Check your spam or junk folder first, and add Cloudflare's notification address to your trusted contacts if it keeps getting filtered.

faq.question: Can I use an expired verification link?
faq.answer: No, an expired link won't work; you need to resend a fresh verification email from your profile settings and use the new link.

faq.question: Does verifying my email affect my domain's DNS settings?
faq.answer: No, email verification is an account-level security step and does not change any of your domain's existing DNS records.
